tasks.title: Solution For Teachers Problem
tasks.description: Practical Answers needed for CRM, Classroom Culture, Feedback, Perseverance etc.
tasks.result: Made a short, practical, research-backed guide with 2-3 'try tomorrow' strategies for each of four areas: Classroom Routines & Management, Classroom Culture, Feedback to students, and Perseverance/Grit/Growth Mindset. Saved to outputs/teacher-solutions-guide.md. NOTE: I read 'CRM' as Classroom Routines & Management (the abbreviation is ambiguous) — tell me if you meant something else and I'll redo that part. Sources are real (Edutopia, IES, PBIS, Frontiers, Stanford/Dweck, etc.).

tasks.title: Draft observation feedback for Ms. Ahmed (Grade 5 Science)
tasks.description: Observed a 40-min water-cycle lesson. Good: opened with a real-world question that hooked students; clear diagram; circulated and checked understanding. To improve: a few quieter students did not get a chance to speak; ran ~5 min over time. Please draft warm, constructive feedback.
tasks.result: Drafted warm, constructive feedback for Ms. Ahmed's Grade 5 Science water-cycle lesson: 3 strengths tied to real moments (real-world hook, clear diagram, circulating to check understanding), one growth area (reaching quieter students, with a turn-and-talk / name-picker step to try), a gentle note on the ~5-min overrun, and an encouraging close. Saved as a DRAFT for you to review and send: outputs/feedback-ms-ahmed-water-cycle.md. Left the observation date as '(add date)'. Ellie does not send it.

tasks.title: Sample: 3 tips for constructive teacher observation feedback
tasks.description: Find 3 well-sourced, practical tips for writing constructive, growth-focused feedback for a teacher after a classroom observation. Include source links.
tasks.result: Researched constructive post-observation feedback and saved 3 sourced tips to outputs/observation-feedback-tips.md. Tips: (1) Anchor feedback in specific observed evidence and lead with a genuine strength; (2) Deliver it promptly, within ~24 hours; (3) Agree on one clear, actionable next step, not a long checklist. Sources: edutopia.org, clickvieweducation.com, kuraplan.com, annenberg.brown.edu.
